summary: PR 2445 decompiled and improved stage switch menu code in src/melee/mn/mnstagesw.c, adding a local MnStageSwData user-data struct, implementing several previously stubbed mnStageSw functions, adding menu navigation/toggle logic, stage icon text creation, JObj animation/state updates, and constructor/setup code. Bot report records two new matches and seven unmatched-item improvements, with one .sdata regression.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; stage switch menu implementation and matching work.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/mn/mnstagesw.c added 577 deleted 17 hunks 6
diff.diff: added MnStageSwData typedef and debug.h include
diff.diff: implemented mnStageSw_802359C8, mnStageSw_80235C58, mnStageSw_80235DC8, fn_80235F80, mnStageSw_80236548, fn_80236998, mnStageSw_80236CBC
text_corpus.jsonl bot report: main/melee/mn/mnstagesw matched/improved items

what_changed.text: Added sysdolphin/baselib/debug.h and used HSD_ASSERTREPORT for MnStageSwData allocation failure handling in mnStageSw_80236CBC.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: +#include <sysdolphin/baselib/debug.h>
diff.diff: HSD_ASSERTREPORT(0x397, data, "Can't get user_data.\n")

what_changed.text: Introduced a local MnStageSwData struct for gobj user_data, including menu state bytes, six HSD_JObj pointers, padding, and HSD_Text pointers for NUM_STAGES.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: typedef struct MnStageSwData { u8 x0; u8 x1; u8 x2[NUM_STAGES]; ... HSD_Text* x40[NUM_STAGES]; }

what_changed.text: Changed mnStageSw_802359C8 signature from HSD_GObj* to MnStageSwData* and implemented stage icon text creation for two columns split at index 15.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: prototype changed to static void mnStageSw_802359C8(MnStageSwData* data)
diff.diff: loops i < 15 and i = 15; i < NUM_STAGES using HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC and HSD_SisLib_803A6368

what_changed.text: Implemented stage selection helper mnStageSw_80235C58 and D-pad navigation handler mnStageSw_80235DC8, including row bounds 0-14 and 15-28 and wrap cases for left/right movement.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: static s32 mnStageSw_80235C58(u8 arg0)
diff.diff: static void mnStageSw_80235DC8(u8* user_data, s32 buttons)
text_corpus.jsonl bot report: mnStageSw_80235DC8 0.00% to 100.00%, +440 bytes

what_changed.text: Implemented fn_80235F80 input handling, stage toggle/save behavior, cancellation/transition behavior, and a narrow PAD_STACK(0x28) tactic.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: static void fn_80235F80(HSD_GObj* gobj)
diff.diff: PAD_STACK(0x28)
diff.diff: buttons & 0x20, 0x200, 0x100, and 0xF branches

what_changed.text: Added noinline wrapper helpers around mnStageSw_80236178 and mnStageSw_802364A0, then used those wrappers in animation/update code.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: static void mnStageSw_80236178_noinline(HSD_GObj* gobj, u8 idx)
diff.diff: static HSD_JObj* mnStageSw_802364A0_noinline(HSD_GObj* gobj, u8 idx)

what_changed.text: Implemented mnStageSw_80236548 and fn_80236998 for cursor visibility, animation frame transfer, highlighted selection movement, menu state transitions, and text cleanup on exit.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: static void mnStageSw_80236548(HSD_GObj* gobj, u8 arg1, u8 arg2)
diff.diff: static void fn_80236998(HSD_GObj* gobj)
text_corpus.jsonl bot report: mnStageSw_80236548 improved to 91.36%; fn_80236998 improved to 82.38%

what_changed.text: Implemented mnStageSw_80236CBC setup code for loading stage switch menu JObjs, allocating user data, initializing stage enabled flags, caching child JObjs, creating cursor JObjs, and positioning them in two columns.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: static HSD_GObj* mnStageSw_80236CBC(s8 arg0)
diff.diff: GObj_Create, HSD_JObjLoadJoint(MenMainConSs_Top.joint), HSD_MemAlloc(sizeof(MnStageSwData)), loops over NUM_STAGES
text_corpus.jsonl bot report: mnStageSw_80236CBC improved to 81.17%, +1522 bytes

smart_moves.text: Converted repeated generated-looking stage loops into indexed for loops over NUM_STAGES where the diff shows only index-dependent accesses.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: mnStageSw_8023593C changed from pointer do-while to for (i = 0; i < NUM_STAGES; i++)
diff.diff: multiple new for loops over NUM_STAGES in fn_80235F80, fn_80236998, mnStageSw_80236CBC

smart_moves.text: Used a local struct to describe gobj->user_data instead of leaving the entire implementation as raw byte/pointer arithmetic.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: MnStageSwData typedef
diff.diff: data = gobj->user_data; data->x1F, data->x2, data->x20..x40 accesses

smart_moves.text: Kept matching tactics narrow and visible: the PR uses dont_inline around mnStageSw_8023593C, explicit noinline wrappers for two helper calls, and one PAD_STACK site.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: #pragma dont_inline on/reset around mnStageSw_8023593C
diff.diff: mnStageSw_80236178_noinline and mnStageSw_802364A0_noinline
diff.diff: PAD_STACK(0x28)

decomp_lessons.lesson: For menu code with GObj user_data, even a partially named local struct can make matching source tractable by grouping known state bytes, cached JObj pointers, padding, and text pointer arrays.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: added MnStageSwData with x0/x1/x2/x1F, x20-x34 JObj pointers, pad_38, x40 text array
diff.diff: functions mnStageSw_802359C8, mnStageSw_80236548, fn_80236998, mnStageSw_80236CBC use MnStageSwData*

decomp_lessons.lesson: Two-column stage-selection UI code may naturally split loops and navigation bounds at index 15, with bounds 0-14 and 15-28 for NUM_STAGES 29.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: mnStageSw_802359C8 first loop i < 15 and second loop i = 15; i < NUM_STAGES
diff.diff: mnStageSw_80235C58 assigns low/high and start/end based on arg0 < 15
diff.diff: mnStageSw_80235DC8 wrap cases 0->14, 15->28, 14->0, 28->15

decomp_lessons.lesson: Bot checkdiff evidence can support narrow match claims, but partial function improvements should be recorded as improvements rather than accepted matches unless the report says 100%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl bot report: mnStageSw_80235DC8 0.00% to 100.00%
text_corpus.jsonl bot report: several functions improved to 75.85%-91.36%, not 100%

naming_conventions.text: The PR preserved address-style function and data names such as mnStageSw_80235DC8, fn_80235F80, mnStageSw_803ED488, and mnStageSw_804D6BF0 where stronger semantic names were not evidenced.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: static function names remain address-style
diff.diff: extern AnimLoopSettings mnStageSw_803ED488[5]; static HSD_GObj* mnStageSw_804D6BF0

naming_conventions.text: Local variables use short role names for clear entities, including gobj, jobj, data, text, buttons, idx, hovered, and selection.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: implemented functions declare HSD_GObj* gobj, HSD_JObj* jobj, MnStageSwData* data, u32 buttons, u8 idx

naming_conventions.text: Struct fields remained offset-like x0/x1/x2/x1F/x20... rather than semantic names, indicating partial type recovery without unsupported meaning.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: MnStageSwData fields named x0, x1, x2, x1F, x20, x24, x28, x2C, x30, x34, pad_38, x40
diff.diff: #pragma dont_inline on before mnStageSw_8023593C and #pragma dont_inline reset after it

Do not infer that all newly implemented functions matched; only mnStageSw_80235DC8 and .sdata2 are listed as new 100% matches in loaded bot evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use partial local structs for menu GObj user_data when fields are known by offset and role
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2445 shows mnstagesw.c becoming more reviewable and matchable by introducing MnStageSwData for gobj->user_data, with known state bytes, JObj pointer slots, padding, and text pointer arrays, while keeping uncertain fields offset-style rather than overnaming them.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: MnStageSwData typedef
diff.diff: functions use data->x1F, data->x2, data->x20..x40

accepted_candidate_records.title: Separate 100% matches from partial improvements in bot reports
accepted_candidate_records.text: In this PR, the bot report lists mnStageSw_80235DC8 as a 100% new match, while several larger functions are only partial improvements. Intake and curation should preserve that distinction.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl bot report: mnStageSw_80235DC8 100.00%
text_corpus.jsonl bot report: mnStageSw_80236CBC 81.17%, mnStageSw_80236548 91.36%, fn_80236998 82.38%

source_update_candidates.title: Document noinline-wrapper tactics as narrow matching exceptions
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2445 uses explicit wrapper helpers named mnStageSw_80236178_noinline and mnStageSw_802364A0_noinline that simply forward to existing helpers. Consider adding guidance that noinline wrapper helpers, like pragmas and PAD_STACK, should remain narrow, named visibly, and backed by objdiff/checkdiff evidence.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: mnStageSw_80236178_noinline forwards to mnStageSw_80236178
diff.diff: mnStageSw_802364A0_noinline returns mnStageSw_802364A0
text_corpus.jsonl bot report: partial improvements in mnstagesw
source_update_candidates.reason: Loaded standards mention pragmas/register/asm and PAD_STACK but do not explicitly name noinline wrapper helpers; this PR provides a concrete example, though intent is not discussed.
